evidence: The finding assumes VAD speech_started gates the STT feed, so a pre-roll buffer would be needed to recover clipped leading syllables. The actual architecture does NOT gate STT by VAD. In voice-agent-session.ts handleUserAudio (lines 527-546), EVERY inbound audio packet is pushed unconditionally to both make.vadAudio AND make.sttAudio in the same call, independent of VAD state: `this.bus.push(Route.Main, make.recordUserAudio(...), make.vadAudio(...), make.sttAudio(...), ...)`. STT therefore receives the continuous, uninterrupted stream from connection open, not just audio after vad.speech_started. The STT plugins confirm continuous streaming: voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:161-175 comment 'Listen for audio packets — stream immediately' then `bus.on('stt.audio', ...) => this.sendAudio(...)` per frame; voice-stt-google/src/index.ts:114-119 likewise sends every stt.audio frame to the provider immediately. handleVadSpeechStarted (lines 620-648) uses speech_started ONLY for filler cancellation, the 'user_started_speaking' event, debug push, and barge-in turn arbitration (turnArbiter.onSpeechStarted) — it never starts/opens the STT feed. The provider (Deepgram/Google streaming) sees all audio and does its own endpointing, so there are no 'first syllables' withheld until VAD fires and nothing to look back/replay. The cited checklist item 'STT-12' does not exist anywhere in the repo (grep for STT-12/look-back/pre-roll/lookback returns only docs/latency-budget.md and webrtc-fec-decision.md, both about the client AudioJitterBuffer playout pre-roll, unrelated to STT input). The look-back/pre-roll pattern kimi describes applies to VAD-segmented STT (local/file-based STT that only transcribes audio between speech_started and speech_ended); this engine uses continuous streaming STT, so the premise does not hold.
real_fix: No fix needed. The premise (STT input begins at vad.speech_started) is false — STT is fed the continuous stream via make.sttAudio on every user.audio_received, independent of VAD. A rolling 500ms PCM pre-roll replayed on vad.speech_started would re-send audio the provider already received, causing duplicated/garbled leading audio. If clipping were ever observed with continuous streaming STT, the cause would be transport ingress dropping frames before connection-ready (check voice-server-websocket ingress + STT conn.ensureReady buffering), not a missing VAD look-back buffer.
